title: ferray-ufunc: binary ops require same shape — broadcast variants are separate functions
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

In NumPy, np.add(a, b) broadcasts automatically. In ferray-ufunc, add() requires same shape and errors otherwise. Broadcasting requires calling add_broadcast() instead, which returns IxDyn. Only add, subtract, multiply, divide have _broadcast variants. All other binary ops (power, remainder, fmod, arctan2, hypot, heaviside, gcd, lcm, all comparisons, all logical ops, all bitwise ops) have no broadcasting at all — they require exact shape match. Either: (1) make all binary ops broadcast by default (like NumPy), or (2) systematically add _broadcast variants for every binary function.
